mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive forward returns occur in less than 55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by over 0.4 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative average forward return or a Rank IC below 0.01 over any 24-month out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints that compress profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in over 58% of observations during tightening periods or fails to achieve statistical significance at the 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of high-inflation months or the signal predictive power vanishes after controlling for prior rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal yields a negative average forward return or a Rank IC below 0.015 over any rolling 36-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates weak household demand despite macro growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive forward returns in over 60% of cases or shows no statistical edge in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates restoring supply chain capacity that lifts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55% of recovery-phase observations or the signal Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 24-month window.
